title: Dashboard Colored Number Widget
excerpt: Could we change the color of the background and the font of the number widget just like the title widget? when we have too many stats on the board it starts looking very 1 dimensional and hard to emphasize on key numbers…

posts.author: jirehly (Jireh Lee)
posts.cookedHtml: Could we change the color of the background and the font of the number widget just like the title widget? when we have too many stats on the board it starts looking very 1 dimensional and hard to emphasize on key numbers.

posts.author: Celine (Céline Vignal)
posts.cookedHtml: I support this request

posts.author: StephaneGout (Stephane Gout)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be great to chose rules as well For example if total > x, make the widget green, if < x make it red

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml: @StephaneGout Yes, it would be great to have Excel-style Conditional Formatting.

posts.author: steyan111 (Andre Steyn)
posts.cookedHtml: I too support custom coloring of dashboard elements

posts.author: Jonatas (Jonatas Santos)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I agree. There is a thread so you can vote and support as well.

Conditional Formatting on Number Widgets Feature requests
It would be great to conditionally format the background or text color on Number widgets based on their value. This could be extended to other widgets as well. Basically duplicate what is available on boards today into Dashboards.

posts.author: Jonatas (Jonatas Santos)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello. Did you get any response from Monday team on this idea?

posts.author: TomDel (Tom Delépine)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I’m bringing back this discussion as it is a critical point for me. Some clients are insisting on the ability to customize the background color of number widgets and other widgets. However, after reviewing two discussions on this request in the forum, we have not received any feedback from Monday. I would like to know if it is now possible to modify these widgets. If not, is there another solution that preserves the quality of these widgets? While I can recreate some widgets myself, it exceeds my current skills as an intern for others.

Thank you in advance for your responses.

posts.author: samgiroux (Sam Giroux)
posts.cookedHtml:
I 100% support this!!

I am curious why monday.com staff have not addressed this concern…it has been requested for 4 years and would take a very minimal amount of engineering.

posts.author: TKingSH (Tami King)
posts.cookedHtml: adding in my vote

posts.author: b2bworld (Ofer Weisglass)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also really like to so see it

moday929×319 26 KB

that i can change each one to other colour

posts.author: JasperDemeurie (Jasper)
posts.cookedHtml:
Second this. I summarize a lot of (in this case dummy) data from several boards into one overview board. Revenue, ad spent, ad budget, customer satisfaction, content scores etc etc.

Al the colleague have their own boards, and for them this is view only.

image1632×777 71.2 KB

Big big fan to be able to change the background on these, based on source.
Customer contact line: pastel red (e.g.).
Content line: pastel green (e.g.)
Sales lines: pastel pink (e.g.).

1 look, and you know what to look for and where.
Now it’s just a bunch of numbers in black squares that you need to go through one by one.

I did read the same question/feature request for board columns as well. Hope they make this available on both places at once.

Someone from Monday?

posts.author: RobaSolutions (Leon from Roba Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d like to bring your attention to “Dashboard Formula Widget”, which was primarily developed for calculating formulas directly within your widget, but also includes the customization features you’re looking for:

  • Customizable font color
  • Adjustable background color
  • Conditional coloring based on values (e.g., red for values below a certain threshold)

While the main purpose is performing calculations, you can also use it like a regular number widget by simply referencing a single number directly in the formula

image901×400 8.73 KB

(as shown in the screenshot with “{NUMBER}”). This way, you get a standard number display with all the additional customization options that are missing in the default widget.

image1037×764 23.8 KB

This might help until an official solution becomes available!
